original_text:
### Modular Assembly Conventions **[GENERAL — Solstice production rules]**
- Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; **no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking.
- Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.
- Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time** — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.
- Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).
- Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).
- All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

relations.note: Declared section backgrounds enable the assembler to prevent adjacent dark-background sections.
Both rules require sections to remain visually independent during direct-stack assembly.
Metadata declares consumed footnote symbols, while assignment occurs at assembly time.

quotes: Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; **no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking.
Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; **no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking.

rules.intent: Ensure modular email sections can be assembled without external spacing dependencies.
rules.rule_text: Each email section is a self-contained table-based block that uses the 600px section canvas, 32px vertical section padding, and zero outside margin; declare its background color and assemble sections by direct stacking.

quotes: Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.

rules.intent: Provide the assembler the section-level information needed to compose modules safely.
rules.rule_text: Each section's metadata must declare its background color, whether it establishes dermatomyositis (DM), the footnote symbols it consumes, and whether it contains a CTA.

sequence: referenced claims in order of appearance
superscript footnote symbols assigned at assembly time

quotes: Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time**

Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time** — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.

rules.intent: Avoid footnote-symbol collisions when independently authored sections are assembled.
rules.rule_text: Footnote symbols must be superscript and assigned in order of appearance at assembly time. When more than one referenced claim exists, sections must use placeholder tokens such as `{{fn1}}` rather than hard-coded daggers.

quotes: Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).
Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).

rules.intent: Keep independently assembled sections visually self-contained and prevent unsafe adjacency combinations.
rules.rule_text: Locked header, footer, and ISI components are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on the section above or below. Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark-background sections.

quotes: All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

rules.intent: Maintain predictable rendering across supported email clients, including Outlook.
rules.rule_text: Email code must express all colors as HEX and all sizing in px, use inline CSS only for email-client compatibility, and duplicate `bgcolor` attributes for Outlook.
